reasoning: The statistical trend model predicts a revert to a trendline value of ~402.4 by early June, putting the probability below 0.5. CHTR's recent deal to acquire Cox Communications drove an initial pop to $427 on May 16, but the stock has experienced consistent downward momentum since, closing at $406.36 on May 23. This negative drift is typical for acquiring companies post-announcement due to anticipated share dilution (Cox will own 23% of the combined entity), integration risks, and potential antitrust scrutiny. The recent price is still significantly elevated compared to its March/April range of $350-$380. Due to these short-term headwinds and mean-reversion expectations, near-term probabilities are weighted below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty increases and the probability of a positive return regresses toward the base rate of 0.5.
